signals.headline: March 2026 CPI data confirmed headline inflation surged 0
signals.raw: March 2026 CPI data confirmed headline inflation surged 0.87% month-over-month, pushing year-over-year headline CPI to 3.29%**[1], with the spike primarily driven by a 10.9% jump in energy prices tied to the Iran war[1]. Oil prices spiked 45% in a single month, jumping from $71 to $103 per barrel between February and March[1].

signals.raw: Market implications:** Goldman has raised its December 2026 headline PCE forecast by a full percentage point since the war began[1], and short-term inflation expectations jumped to 4.8% in the University of Michigan survey[1]. signals.headline: Key uncertainty:** Goldman's oil strategists expect Brent crude to pull back to $80 by Q4 2026, but explicitly note upsi
signals.raw: Key uncertainty:** Goldman's oil strategists expect Brent crude to pull back to $80 by Q4 2026, but explicitly note upside risks remain[1], making the inflation trajectory heavily dependent on Iran conflict resolution.
